mission.title: harbor: Quota of dockerhub is still used in v2.1.1 after the image is cached.
mission.description: Quota of dockerhub is still used in v2.1.1 after the image is cached.. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current harbor deployment
steps.description:
Verify your harbor version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
helm list -n harbor 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working harbor installation.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
Was reported by Remoco Breekveldt from slack channel:

1. Harbor Version v2.1.1-5f52168e with Proxy cache configured on example.com/docker-hub
2.

steps.title: Check and adjust quota configuration
steps.description:
Review and adjust the proxy cache project quota settings:
```bash
# Check the current quota for the proxy cache project
curl -u admin:<HARBOR_PASSWORD> \
  'https://<harbor-host>/api/v2.0/quotas' | jq '.[] | select(.ref.name=="<proxy-project>")'

# Check storage usage
curl -u admin:<HARBOR_PASSWORD> \
  'https://<harbor-host>/api/v2.0/projects/<proxy-project>/summary'
```
Upgrade Harbor to a version that correctly handles manifest list quota accounting for proxy cache projects, or increase the project quota to accommodate the overhead.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Quota of dockerhub is still used in v2.1.1 after the image…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: After an image is cached via proxy cache, the quota for the upstream registry (e.g., Docker Hub) continues to be consumed because Harbor counts both the cached copy and the upstream reference. The issue is compounded by manifest lists (multi-arch images), which are common on Docker Hub. The fix involves properly accounting for cached manifest lists so that quota is only consumed for the locally stored layers, not the full upstream manifest list size.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
